manifest.name: vibgyor

name: vibgyor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# v1.1.0

## ✨ New Features
- **Pattern Color Customization:** You can now set an independent `pattern-color` via frontmatter or the theme modal, decoupling the background pattern color from your text color.
- **Pattern Scale Slider:** Added a real-time slider in the theme modal to adjust the size and density of any background pattern (saves to `pattern-scale` frontmatter).
- **Release Notes Modal:** A new update notification popup that shows what's new after updates (can be toggled in Settings -> Preferences).

## 🐛 Fixes & Improvements
- Fixed background pattern shifting position when scrolling in long notes.
- Pattern color now falls back to pen color when not explicitly set.
- Theme presets now support custom `patternColor` and `patternScale` fields.
- Cleaned up the settings tab UI by removing redundant headers.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# 🌈 vibgyor v1.0.2

This patch release focuses on visual brand refinement, a clearer tagline description, and internal script improvements to ensure future updates are published seamlessly.

## 🚀 What's New

* **Sleeker Brand Presentation**: Refined the display name of the plugin from `v i b g y o r` to a clean, cohesive **`vibgyor`** (the internal plugin ID remains unchanged).
* **Clearer Value Proposition**: Updated the main tagline to better highlight the core features:
  > *"Customize your notes with custom color palettes and built-in background patterns."*

## 🛠️ Under the Hood

* **Version Bump Automation Fix**: Resolved a logic bug in `version-bump.mjs` that prevented new versions with shared `minAppVersion` requirements from being written to `versions.json`. Future releases will now be cataloged automatically.
* **Compatibility Mapping**: Registered version `1.0.2` in `versions.json` for full compatibility with Obsidian `1.4.4` and above.

---

### 📦 Installation
If installing manually:
1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the assets below.
2. Place them in your `.obsidian/plugins/vibgyor/` directory.
3. Enable **vibgyor** in your Community Plugins settings!

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed in v1.0.1

### 🚀 Features & Compatibility
* **Obsidian Popout Window Support**: Replaced `activeDocument.createElement` and `window.setTimeout` with Obsidian's native `createDiv()` utility and `activeWindow.setTimeout`. This ensures that themes, custom colors, and backgrounds render correctly inside secondary popout windows.

### 🛠️ Developer Experience & CI/CD
* **ESLint Configuration Fixes**: 
  - Excluded `eslint.config.mts` from the parser to prevent Espree compatibility/parsing errors during build checks.
  - Defined globals (`activeWindow`, `createDiv`) to prevent lint warnings.
  - Upgraded paths to use standard Node `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` logic for `tsconfigRootDir` resolution.
* **Version Management**: Bumped the plugin version to `1.0.1` in `manifest.json`, `package.json`, and `versions.json`.

---
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ZeroDark-0/vIbGyOr/compare/1.0.0...1.0.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🎨 v i b g y o r > v1.0.0

The first official release of **v i b g y o r** > customize your Obsidian notes with unique colors and patterns.

### ✨ Features

- **Theme Presets** > Curated color themes across Minimal and Advanced categories
- **Custom Palettes** > Define your own page, pen, link, and accent colors per note
- **Page Patterns** > 12 background patterns including Lined, Dotted, Grid, Cornell, Blueprint, Woven, Hexagonal, Cosmos, Starfield, Zen Waves, Cyber Maze, and Cyber Circuit
- **Image Recoloring** > Automatically tints transparent-background images to match your note's pen color, with a toggle to view originals
- **Per-Note Theming** > Themes are stored in frontmatter, so each note can have its own look
- **Create & Edit** > Create new themed notes or edit the theme of existing ones via ribbon icons, command palette, or right-click menu
